brand.name: Actalent Services
brand.vertical: B2B/professional — engineering & sciences staffing and technical services

offer.what: Engineering and sciences staffing (contract, contract-to-hire, direct placement) plus project-based technical services (engineering services, clinical development, scientific support, project/quality management consulting)
offer.coreOutcome: Enterprises get the specialized engineering and sciences talent they need to execute complex projects on time; candidates get career-defining placements in high-impact roles
offer.pricePoint: Not publicly disclosed; staffing markup model (percentage of contractor bill rate) for talent solutions; project-based SOW pricing for technical services — both are enterprise-tier B2B contracts
offer.commitment: Varies: contract placements (weeks-to-years), direct hire (one-time fee), project SOW (defined scope/duration) — no self-serve; all require sales engagement
leadMagnets: Industry case studies and success stories
Whitepapers (clinical trial management, infrastructure risk, etc.)
Insights blog (engineering/sciences career and market content)

audience.icp: Engineering directors, VP-level R&D and operations leaders, and HR/talent acquisition managers at mid-to-large enterprises in regulated industries (life sciences, aerospace, utilities, transportation, semiconductor) who have active hiring gaps or project resource shortfalls
painPoints: Critical skills gaps slowing project delivery
Long time-to-fill for niche engineering and scientific roles
Compliance and quality risk in regulated environments (FDA, FAA, etc.)
Inability to scale headcount quickly for project spikes
High cost and risk of mis-hires in specialized disciplines
segments: Employer / client buyers (talent acquisition, engineering leadership, project management office)
Engineering job seekers (early-career to senior engineers seeking contract or perm roles)
Sciences / clinical candidates (CRAs, lab scientists, clinical research coordinators)
Current contractors on active placements (retention and expansion target)

salesCycle.length: Weeks to months for new enterprise clients; days-to-weeks for repeat clients with active requisitions
salesCycle.trial: Free consultation — contact form routes to a sales rep; no structured free trial or demo product
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Inbound inquiry → sales rep qualification call → proposal/SOW → contract signature; closed by a human account manager
salesCycle.topObjection: Staffing markup cost vs. direct hire or offshore alternative; trust that Actalent's talent pool quality justifies the margin

ahaMoment: For employer clients: the first qualified candidate submittal that converts to a productive placement. For candidates: receiving a matched job opportunity that fits their discipline and career goals within days of registering.

retention.recurring: Ongoing staffing contracts (rolling contractor placements, master service agreements) and repeat project SOWs — revenue is largely relationship-recurring rather than subscription
retention.expansion: Adding new requisition types, expanding into adjacent service lines (e.g., client using talent solutions adds engineering services), or increasing contractor headcount on existing accounts
retention.churnRisk: Client switches to competing staffing firm or builds internal recruiting capacity; contractors accept a direct offer from client (conversion) or go dormant between placements

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & onboarding sequence for new client contacts (no evidence of automated nurture post-inquiry)
Lead-magnet nurture series leveraging whitepapers/market reports to move prospects down funnel
Trial/consult conversion sequence following initial consultation call
Candidate engagement and re-engagement (between active placements — dormant talent pool)
Renewal / contract extension campaign before placement end dates
Win-back for lapsed client accounts that haven't submitted a requisition in 90+ days
toneVoice: Professional, forward-looking, and solution-oriented with a human touch — balances authoritative industry expertise with accessible, people-first language. Avoids heavy jargon; emphasizes partnership, progress, and measurable outcomes. Moderately formal (business-to-business) but warm enough to speak to individual contributors and candidates.
compliance: CAN-SPAM / CASL compliant required; staffing industry touches employment law — avoid guarantees around placement timelines or compensation. No HIPAA or financial-sector constraints identified, but life sciences content should be careful around clinical claims. Standard B2B unsubscribe and consent practices apply.
